pattern: Every reachable total in the two jugs is an integer combination of the two capacities, so by Bezout's identity a target is achievable exactly when it is a multiple of their greatest common divisor and does not exceed their combined capacity.
hints: Think about which totals are even reachable: each operation adds or removes a whole jug's worth, so the attainable amounts are sums and differences of the two capacities.
The set of integers expressible as multiples of two numbers is precisely the multiples of their greatest common divisor.

Return true when the target is at most the sum of the two capacities and the target is divisible by gcd(x, y); also accept the trivial cases where the target is zero or equals a single capacity or their sum.

edge_inputs.description: the Die Hard pour sequence reaches four
target not a multiple of the gcd is impossible
target equals the sum of both jugs
target equals a single jug capacity
coprime jugs can reach any value up to their sum
gcd greater than one blocks values between its multiples
equal jugs only reach multiples of that capacity

stress.description: random capacities and targets so the gcd-divisibility rule is exercised across many cases
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    x = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    y = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    target = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [x, y, target]
stress.note: free draws within bounds hit both reachable and unreachable targets

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def canMeasureWater(self, x, y, target):
        if target > x + y:
            return False
        seen = set()
        stack = [(0, 0)]
        while stack:
            a, b = stack.pop()
            if a == target or b == target or a + b == target:
                return True
            if (a, b) in seen:
                continue
            seen.add((a, b))
            stack.append((x, b))
            stack.append((a, y))
            stack.append((0, b))
            stack.append((a, 0))
            pour = min(a, y - b)
            stack.append((a - pour, b + pour))
            pour = min(b, x - a)
            stack.append((a + pour, b - pour))
        return False
